name: Taq DNA Polymerase
striking_fact: Stays liquid at −20°C because it’s in 50% glycerol — take it out, use it, return it immediately.

name: Ethanol, 200 proof (absolute)
striking_fact: Vapor pressure is high enough to cause volume drift on every aspirate — pre-wet tips 3× before dispensing.

name: Glycerol, 99%+ (anhydrous)
striking_fact: 1400× more viscous than water — standard pipetting speed causes air bubble entrapment and large volume errors.

name: Tween-20 (Polysorbate 20)
striking_fact: Even 0.1% Tween-20 foams aggressively if dispensed with air — always dispense along the tube wall with no air gap.

name: DAPI (4′,6-Diamidino-2-phenylindole dihydrochloride)
striking_fact: DAPI photobleaches rapidly under UV — even 30 seconds on the UV transilluminator before imaging will kill your signal.

name: DTT (Dithiothreitol, DL-Dithiothreitol)
striking_fact: DTT in solution oxidizes and loses reducing power within ~1 week at 4°C — make fresh on the day of use for sensitive applications.

name: Paraformaldehyde (PFA), solid
striking_fact: PFA releases formaldehyde gas (IARC Group 1 carcinogen) during dissolution — always prepare and use in a fume hood.

name: DMSO (Dimethyl sulfoxide)
striking_fact: DMSO freezes at 19°C and is solid in a 4°C cold room — always warm to room temperature before pipetting.

name: Polyclonal IgG Antibody (generic, dilute)
striking_fact: Dilute antibodies adsorb irreversibly to standard plastic — use LoBind tubes and never let concentration drop below 10 µg/mL without adding BSA carrier.

name: Sodium Hydroxide (NaOH), pellets
striking_fact: NaOH pellets can absorb enough moisture from humid air to dissolve themselves in minutes — weigh quickly and cap immediately.
